qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Binary data endpoint missing authorization allows any authenticated user to read any workflow output file
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: missing authorization on GET /rest/binary-data/, not a Python Code node sandbox escape through insufficiently restricted Python built-ins.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Environment variable exposure via workflow expressions in default configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Superficially overlaps on authenticated workflow editing and Code node/expression contexts, but the described root cause is default $env/process.env exposure in expression evaluation, not the advisory's Python Code node sandbox escape via Python built-in objects under Task Runners.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery in workflow import-from-url endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: unrestricted axios.get(query.url) in GET /rest/workflows/from-url, not Python Code node sandbox restriction failure.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an authenticated n8n Python Code node sandbox escape when Task Runners are enabled, caused by insufficient restriction of certain built-in Python objects and leading to arbitrary file read or RCE. The final qualifying findings identify different root causes: binary-data endpoint authorization bypass, default workflow expression environment exposure, and SSRF in the workflow import-from-url endpoint. None identifies the advisory-described Python sandbox code path or an equivalent vulnerable source-to-sink/control-flow condition.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-mmgg-m5j7-f83h: Python Code node sandbox escape via insufficiently restricted Python built-in objects, requiring workflow create/modify permission and N8N_RUNNERS_ENABLED=true.
binary-data-idor targets GET /rest/binary-data/ and a missing per-user authorization check before binaryDataService.getAsStream(binaryDataId).
env-exposure-default-config targets process.env exposure through workflow expressions and the $env proxy when N8N_BLOCK_ENV_ACCESS_IN_NODE is not set to true.
ssrf-workflow-import targets GET /rest/workflows/from-url and unrestricted axios.get(query.url).
